title: Tanker report - Week 18
summary:
Clean
In the Middle East Gulf the LR market has continued to drive upwards this week. LR2s of TC1 75k Middle East Gulf / Japan climbed 51.43 points to WS288.57 returning just under $60,000 /day round trip TCE. The LR1s have also pushed but with lesser traction.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: In the Middle East Gulf the LR market has continued to drive upwards this week. LR2s of TC1 75k Middle East Gulf / Japan climbed 51.43 points to WS288.57 returning just under $60,000 /day round trip TCE.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The VLCC sector continued on the downward path with 280,000mt Middle East Gulf/USG (via Cape of Good Hope) being assessed about 1.5 points lower than last week to slightly below WS24.5. In the 270,000mt Middle East Gulf/China market rates fell almost two points to WS44.7 (a round trip TCE of minus $8,300 per day).

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Rates for the 135,000mt Novorossiysk/Augusta fell a further 25 points this week and the latest assessment is at the WS145 level (a round-trip TCE of $41,300 per day). Overnight reports detail an Italian charterer fixing at WS135 for a voyage similar to TD6.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The 80,000mt Ceyhan/Mediterranean market saw an improvement this week, recovering eight points to around the WS157.5-160 region (a round-trip TCE of $24,500 per day). In Northern Europe the rate for 80,000mt Hound Point/UK Continent were flat at the WS155 level (a round-trip TCE of $22,600 per day).
